title: scutellaria pekinensis var. pekinensis
intro: Scutellaria pekinensis var. pekinensis, also known as Chinese skullcap, is a plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to China and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, and has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Chinese skullcap include reducing inflammation and improving liver function, as well as having antioxidant and neuroprotective effects. Chinese skullcap can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance its therapeutic effects.
